purpose: Students recognize that vertical and horizontal graphs show the same data—the orientation changes but the math doesn't.
visual:
Same data shown two ways side-by-side:
  - Left: Vertical bar graph (scale of 10; includes at least one between-lines value at 35)
  - Right: Horizontal bar graph (same data, same scale)
  - Category with value 35 highlighted in both graphs
guide: "Here's the same data shown two ways—vertical and horizontal. Find the category with 35 in BOTH graphs."
student_action: Click on the 35 bar in vertical graph, then in horizontal graph
on_correct: "Same value, same position between the lines—just different directions."
remediation_light: "Find 35 on the axis in each graph. Which bar ends there?"
connection_guide_states_after_task: "The axis works the same way whether bars go up or sideways. The math doesn't change—only the direction."
